query:
You are a competitive intelligence analyst building a deep benchmark profile of Freebe (https://freebe.me/).

Search https://freebe.me/, https://freebe.me//pricing, https://freebe.me//plans, Crunchbase, LinkedIn, G2, Capterra, TechCrunch, and recent news. Run several searches with adjacent keywords to ensure full breadth.

Extract the following — include a source URL for each section:

PRICING: Visit https://freebe.me//pricing and https://freebe.me//plans. List every plan name, exact monthly USD price, exact annual USD price, free tier availability (true/false), any recent pricing changes.

FUNDING: Search Crunchbase and news for 'Freebe funding'. Extract: funding stage, latest round amount USD, round date (YYYY-MM-DD), round type (Seed/Series A/B/C/etc), total raised USD, all investor names.

COMPANY: Find the LinkedIn company page URL for Freebe. Extract current employee count. Note any headcount growth signals from LinkedIn or news.

FOUNDERS: Find CEO and founder names for Freebe. Extract their LinkedIn profile URLs. Search for recent LinkedIn posts or press statements from founders (last 6 months) — extract the key signal or quote for each.

SIGNALS: Search news and press from the last 6 months about Freebe. For each item extract: date, headline, signal type (funding/product/hiring/partnership/press), source URL.

POSITIONING: List key differentiators of Freebe vs alternatives. Identify primary target customer segment. List up to 5 publicly known customer names.

WEAKNESSES: Search G2, Capterra, Reddit, Hacker News for user complaints or limitations of Freebe. List the most recurring themes.

You are a product analyst building a feature comparison matrix. The subject is the subject company (). Compare it against the 10 competitors below.

Competitors:
  1. name="Nalo", website="https://nalo.fr"
  2. name="Linxo", website="https://linxo.com"
  3. name="Grisbee", website="https://grisbee.com"
  4. name="Horiz.io", website="https://horiz.io"
  5. name="Sharesight", website="https://sharesight.com"
  6. name="InvMon", website="https://invmon.com"
  7. name="Wealthfolio", website="https://ealthfolio.app"
  8. name="Quicken", website="https://quicken.com"
  9. name="Merlin", website="https://merlin.com"
  10. name="Sure", website="https://sure.com"

STEP 1 — Identify 6 to 10 KEY FEATURES that matter when comparing these products. Group them by category (Core / Pricing / Integrations / Enterprise / Mobile). Return as 'features' array with {label, group}. The SAME features list must be used to score every competitor — choose features that are meaningful for all.

STEP 2 — For EACH competitor, return a 'capabilities' array with one entry per feature defined in step 1. Each entry: {feature: '<exact label>', value: '<one of full/part/none/soon>'}. 'full' = covered with a dedicated feature. 'part' = partial / limited coverage. 'none' = not supported. 'soon' = on public roadmap.

STEP 3 — For EACH competitor, also return notable_customers: 3-5 publicly known customers with {name, domain, segment (Grand compte/ETI/PME/Startup/Consumer), industry, evidence (case study URL or quote)}.

Omit any capability you cannot verify — do NOT guess. Return one competitor object per company in the SAME order as the input list. Preserve company names EXACTLY.

- geo_coverage: pick one of: Local (1 city/country), National, Regional (continent), Global

Omit any field you cannot verify — do NOT invent classifications. Return one object per company in the SAME order as the input list. Preserve company names EXACTLY.

Build a comprehensive competitive intelligence profile for the company listed below. Return facts only, not reasoning.

Companies:
1. Paradox (https://www.paradox.ai)

Gather the following from public sources (company website, Crunchbase, LinkedIn, TechCrunch, G2, Capterra, press releases). For EACH company extract:

IDENTITY
- name (EXACT match from list above — CRITICAL for matching)
- website domain, tagline (one-sentence elevator pitch)
- category (primary market, e.g. 'HR Technology'), sub_category (specific niche)
- linkedin_url (linkedin.com/company/<slug>)
- hq_city, hq_country, founded_year
- employee_count (LinkedIn About) and employee_growth_yoy as decimal (0.12 = +12%)

POSITIONING
- business_model: B2B, B2C, B2B2C, Marketplace, or API/Platform
- gtm_motion: sales-led, product-led, marketing-led, or community-led
- geo_coverage: Local, National, Regional, or Global
- target_segment: Enterprise, Mid-Market, SMB, Consumer, or Mixed
- target_verticals (3-5 industries)
- top_3_features, key_differentiator (max 20 words)

PRICING (from /pricing or /plans page)
- pricing_model_kind: Freemium, Subscription, Usage-based, Enterprise, or Hybrid
- pricing.free_plan (bool), starts_at_usd (0 if custom), mention
- pricing.tiers: array of {name, price_monthly_usd, price_annual_usd, target, features[]}

FUNDING (Crunchbase + press releases)
- funding_stage, funding_total_usd
- funding_rounds: array of {round, date YYYY-MM-DD, amount_usd, lead}
- notable_investors: array of {name, domain}
- acquisition: {acquired (bool), acquirer, year, amount_usd, source_url}

TRACTION (only if explicitly disclosed in press or filings)
- arr_usd, customer_count, avg_contract_usd — omit if not stated, do NOT estimate

TEAM
- key_people: top 5 senior CURRENT employees from LinkedIn People tab. For each: name, current role AT THIS COMPANY, linkedin URL (linkedin.com/in/<slug>), one-sentence background starting with current role at this company. Include only people currently employed at this company — exclude external partners, advisors, event speakers.

CUSTOMERS
- notable_customers: 5-10 customers mentioned on company website or case studies. For each: name, domain, segment (Grand compte/ETI/PME/Startup/Consumer), industry, evidence (quote / case-study URL / logo placement)

SIGNALS (last 12 months)
- recent_news: 5 most recent press items {date YYYY-MM-DD, headline, source_url}
- growth_signals: 3-5 one-sentence growth events
- weaknesses: top 3 user complaints from G2 / Capterra / Reddit

OUTPUT FORMAT
Return JSON {"competitors": [...]} with one entry per company, preserving the input ORDER and EXACT company names. If a field cannot be retrieved, OMIT it from the response — do NOT pad with null or placeholder values. Do not fabricate data.

You are a SaaS pricing analyst. For each of the 1 companies below, extract their CURRENT pricing structure from their public pricing pages ({domain}/pricing, {domain}/plans), G2, Capterra, and reverse-engineering posts.

Companies:
  1. name="Linear", website="linear.app"

For EACH company extract:
- pricing_model_kind: one of Freemium, Subscription, Usage-based, Enterprise, Hybrid
- free_plan: true/false
- starts_at_usd: lowest paid tier monthly USD (0 if free plan only, null if Enterprise-only / contact sales)
- mention: short UI tagline e.g. 'Starts at $20/user/mo' or 'Contact sales' or 'Free + paid plans from $8/mo'
- tiers: array of {name, price_monthly_usd, price_annual_usd, features (3-6 strings), target}. Include ALL public tiers (e.g. Free, Pro, Business, Enterprise). For Enterprise tiers without public price, set price_monthly_usd=null and target='Enterprise'.
- recent_changes: any pricing change in last 12 months (date + source). Leave blank if none.

Omit any field you cannot verify — do NOT invent prices. Return one object per company in the SAME order as the input list. Preserve company names EXACTLY.
